steps.title: Read the pupil-size pattern
steps.content: The study starts from the idea that humans view dilated pupils positively. Because dark irises can make dogs' pupils look larger, the relevant data pattern is whether dogs with large-appearing pupils received more positive human ratings than dogs whose irises did not create that effect.

steps.title: Match the data claim
steps.content: Choice A reverses the pattern by saying participants responded more negatively. Choice B denies a relationship between iris shade and responses. Choice D compares actual and apparent pupil size, a distinction the described study does not establish. The supported claim is that positive responses extend to dogs with large-appearing pupils. The answer is C.
